settings.title: Settings
settings.clearCacheMessage: Are you sure? This will remove temporary images.

center.name: Symbols of Volgograd
center.description: A route through the city’s landmarks: from Motherland Calls on Mamayev Kurgan to the riverside amphitheater and the interactive history park. A good introduction to Volgograd’s architecture and culture in one day.

history.name: Road of memory
history.description: A path through memorials of the Battle of Stalingrad: Lysaya Gora, the Military Echelon near the panorama, and the first tram monument. Ideal for a themed walk focused on the city’s military history.

poetic.name: Poetic Volgograd
poetic.description: Lysaya Gora, the Volga amphitheater, and the Military Echelon: war memory, embankment architecture, and railway heritage in one route.

1.name: The Motherland Calls
1.description: The Motherland Calls is a statue on Mamayev Kurgan...

2.name: Memorial Complex "Lysaya Gora" (Bald Hill)
2.description: The memorial complex on Lysaya Gora (Bald Hill) in Volgograd marks the site of fierce battles during the Battle of Stalingrad. The 145.5 m high hill had strategic importance: months‑long battles were fought for it in 1942–1943. In memory of the Soviet soldiers’ feat, a memorial was opened here in 1968. The central element of the complex is a 20 m obelisk, with mass graves and a display area of WWII military equipment nearby. The memorial serves as a reminder of the heroism of Stalingrad’s defenders and is a place of remembrance and patriotic education.
2.main_elements: Obelisk, mass graves, military equipment display area

3.name: Military Train Memorial
3.location: Volgograd, Russia (near the "Stalingrad Battle" Panorama Museum)
3.description: The "Military Train" is a memorial complex near the "Stalingrad Battle" Panorama Museum in Volgograd. The monument honours railway workers and military builders who ensured uninterrupted supply of military cargoes to the Stalingrad Front during the Battle of Stalingrad. The complex opened on June 24, 2009. It includes a historic Er 743‑64 steam locomotive (built in 1935 at the Luhansk Locomotive Works) and pre‑war wagons: a covered wagon ("teplushka"), an oil tanker, and an ambulance wagon. Historical railway infrastructure elements are also on display: a 1938 semaphore and a steam locomotive water‑filling column. The exhibition clearly demonstrates the role of railway transport in combat operations and wounded evacuation during WWII.
3.main_elements: Steam locomotive, teplushka (covered wagon), oil tanker, ambulance wagon

4.name: Tram Monument
4.description: The Tram Monument is a reconstructed tram car of the Series X, installed in Komsomolsky Garden in Volgograd. The monument’s opening on April 19, 2013 marked the 100th anniversary of tram service launch in the city: in 1913, Tsaritsyn became the first county town in Russia to have an electric tram. The reconstruction was based on an MS‑4 wagon from 1932, brought from St. Petersburg. The tram’s exterior and interior were recreated from historical photos. The car uses materials typical of the early 20th century. Series X trams operated in Tsaritsyn‑Stalingrad‑Volgograd from 1929 to 1967. The monument stands on a special track platform paved with cobblestones.

5.name: Volgograd Amphitheatre
5.description: The Volgograd Amphitheatre is a modern multifunctional venue and one of the city’s key landmarks, located on the banks of the Volga River. The architectural design is inspired by ancient models: the semicircular colonnade and balustrades are made of high‑strength Dagestan stone. There are 52 columns in total. From the stage, there is a panoramic view of the Volga, the Dancing Bridge, and the lower terrace of the Central Embankment. The venue has become Volgograd’s main youth platform and hosts major city events.

6.name: Historical Park Russia — My History
6.location: Volgograd, Russia (Naberezhnaya 62nd Armii St., 1b, in the Tsaritsa River floodplain)
6.description: The Historical Park "Russia — My History" in Volgograd is an interactive museum opened in 2017 as part of a nationwide project. It is a multimedia exhibition complex over 7 000 sq. m in area, using modern technologies to tell the story of Russia from 862 to 2016. The exposition includes four multimedia exhibitions: "Rurik Dynasty", "Romanovs", "From Great Upheavals to Great Victory. 1914–1945", and "Russia — My History. 1945–2016". The museum features touch tables and screens, interactive panoramas, video infographics, animation, 3D modeling, and digital reconstructions. The venue also hosts lectures, film screenings, festivals, temporary exhibitions, and historical quests for children and adults.
6.main_exhibitions: Rurikids, Romanovs, From Great Upheavals to Great Victory, Russia - My History. 1945–2016
6.technologies: touch screens, interactive tables, 3D models, video projections, animation, digital reconstructions
6.schedule: Tue–Fri: 10:00–18:00; Sat–Sun: 12:00–20:00; Mon is a day off
6.ticket_prices: Adults: 200 RUB; Concessions: 100 RUB; Children under 7 years old, military personnel, and disabled people of groups I and II: free of charge
